headline: LinkedIn co-founder Reid Hoffman to step down from Microsoft's board
summary: Reid Hoffman, billionaire co-founder of networking website LinkedIn, has ‌decided not to stand for re-election ‌to Microsoft's board of directors at its 2026 ​annual shareholder meeting, the software giant said in a filing on Friday.  Hoffman, who has served on the board since 2017, ‌will stay on ⁠till the meeting.  In 2016, ​he sold LinkedIn ​to Microsoft for $26.2 ‌billion in what was at the time the tech giant's largest-ever deal.

content.title: LinkedIn co-founder Reid Hoffman to step down from Microsoft's board
content.summary: Reid Hoffman, billionaire co-founder of networking website LinkedIn, has ‌decided not to stand for re-election ‌to Microsoft's board of directors at its 2026 ​annual shareholder meeting, the software giant said in a filing on Friday.  Hoffman, who has served on the board since 2017, ‌will stay on ⁠till the meeting.  In 2016, ​he sold LinkedIn ​to Microsoft for $26.2 ‌billion in what was at the time the tech giant's largest-ever deal.

headline: Cloud AI Update - Devenex Launches Governance Infrastructure for Enterprise AI Agents
summary: Devenex has introduced a new governance infrastructure intended to manage the execution of AI agents within enterprises, highlighting a key development in the Cloud AI sector. As AI agents increasingly perform critical tasks such as modifying financial records and approving workflows, there is a growing need for robust oversight mechanisms. This infrastructure, launched at Google Cloud Next 2026, provides enterprises with a control layer that ensures all AI-initiated actions are governed...

headline: DocuSign Stock At 11x Earnings — Bargain Or Value Trap?
summary: DocuSign (DOCU) reported a solid earnings beat for Q1 FY2027, delivering $830.2 million in revenue and $1.09 in non-GAAP earnings per share. The company generated $289 million in free cash flow at a 35% margin. Yet, despite these top-line metrics, the stock closed at $50.95 during regular trading and slid nearly 4% to approximately $49 in extended trading. This price implies just 11 times its expected FY2027 earnings of $4.44 per share, significantly lower than the historical software sector ave
